name: Prof. A. Seshadri Sekhar
biosketch:
Dr.  A. Seshadri Sekhar is currently the Director of IIT Palakkad, Kerala.  He received his degrees, B.E (1983) from Andhra University, M.E (1988) from IISc Bangalore and Ph.D (1993) from IIT Madras, before joining as a visiting lecturer in 1993 at IIT Kharagpur and became Professor in 2003. After serving at IIT Kharagpur for 13 years, he has been a Professor at IIT Madras since 2006. He was the Head (2020-22) of the Mechanical Engineering department at the Indian Institute of Technology, Madras. Before that he was the Professor-in-charge of Central Workshop (2018-20). He also served (1999-2000) as the Organising Vice-Chairman, GATE-2000 at IIT Kharagpur. 

He was awarded the Carrier Award for Young Teacher by the All-India Council of Technical Education (AICTE) in 1998. He did research at TU Darmstadt, Germany as an Alexander von Humboldt Research Fellow in 2002. He has published 206 Technical papers including 103 international journals. He has guided 13 Ph.D students. He is the co-author of the book –Dynamic Analysis of Rotating Systems and Applications (Multi Science Publishing Ltd., UK). Currently, he is an Associate editor of Journal Vibration Engineering and Technologies, of Springer Nature. He has served as an editorial member of the Journal of Advances in Mechanical Engineering (SAGE Publishers).  He has significantly contributed to the fields such as rotor dynamics, condition monitoring and vibrations.  He had also worked as an Engineer, for about 4 years in NTPC and BEML, before settling in the teaching profession.  He is currently a member of Vibration and Mechanical Shock Sectional Committee, Bureau of Indian Standards since 2016. Prof. Sekhar has been featured consecutively  in all four versions so far of the Top 2% Scientists list (<https://elsevier.digitalcommonsdata.com/datasets/btchxktzyw/7>) released by Stanford University.

name: Dr. Afzaal Ahmed
biosketch:
Dr. Afzaal Ahmed is an Associate Professor in Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Palakkad. His research interests broadly centers on the non-conventional machining, hybrid machining, ultra-precision machining, deep hole drilling, metal additive manufacturing (AM) and post-processing of additive manufactured components. His research expertise also includes evaluation of surfaces via roughness, microhardness, microstructure with various microscopic and spectroscopic techniques like SEM, EDS, XRD, AFM, TEM and XPS.

Prior to joining IIT Palakkad, he served as a Research Fellow at the Advanced Manufacturing Laboratory (AML) in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at NUS Singapore. During this time, he was instrumental in securing funding from A\*Star Singapore and SIMTech Singapore for the development and implementation of hybrid processing techniques for Large Format Machining of Nickel-based superalloys, particularly Inconel 718 for the Oil and Gas Industry. During his PhD, he has developed a novel hybrid electrical discharge and arc machining process (HEDAM) for the efficient and ultrafast machining of difficult-to-cut superalloys such as Inconel 718, Ti-6Al-4V etc. After joining IIT Palakkad, he has secured funds from the Science & Engineering Research Board (SERB), Federal Bank and a Seed Grant & Exploratory Research Grant (ERG) from IIT Palakkad to support the research activities in the [PEARL Group](https://sites.google.com/view/pearl-group-iitpkd) (Dr. Afzaal’s Research Group).

**Educational Background**

**PhD**- Department of Mechanical Engineering, National University of Singapore (NUS), Singapore.

**M.Tech** - Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, (W.B), India.

**B.E** -  Mechanical Engineering, Pt. Ravi Shankar Shukla University (Pt.RSSU), Raipur, (C.G),  India.

**Work Experience**

Worked as Research Fellow in Advanced Manufacturing Laboratory (AML)  Department of Mechanical Engineering at NUS Singapore. The post-doc position involved working on an A\*Star Singapore funded project, Large Format Machining of Inconel 718 for Oil and Gas Industry.

name: Dr. Anish Hirwe
biosketch:
Anish Hirwe is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad (Jan 2023 onward). My key research interest is in the intersection of Computer Systems, Networks and Security.

**Current Focus**

I am actively working on research problems related to:

* High-performance networking and observability
* Programmable data planes and custom network behavior
* Container security
* Network telemetry in distributed systems for fine-grained monitoring and diagnostics

name: Dr. Arvind Ajoy
biosketch:
* Post-doctoral researcher in the group of [Prof. Debdeep Jena](https://djena.engineering.cornell.edu/) and [Prof. Grace Xing](http://gracexing.research.engineering.cornell.edu/), first at University of Notre Dame (2013-2014) and then at Cornell University (2015).
* Post-doctoral researcher with Prof. Gerhard Klimeck in the [Nanoelectronic Modeling Group](https://engineering.purdue.edu/gekcogrp/) at [Purdue University](http://www.purdue.edu/) from  2012 to 2013.
* PhD in [Electrical Engineering](http://www.ee.iitm.ac.in/) from the [Indian Institute of Technology Madras](http://www.iitm.ac.in/). (thesis submitted in August 2012, defended in June 2013). My PhD advisor was [Prof. Shreepad Karmalkar](http://www.ee.iitm.ac.in/~karmal).
* Co-op at the IBM Semiconductor Research and Development Center, Bangalore, India in the group of [Dr. Kota Murali](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kota-murali-39290b4) from May 2010 -- May 2012.
* BTech degree with a major in Electrical Engineering, and a minor in Physics from IIT Madras in July 2006.

name: Dr. Avirup Mandal
biosketch: I am a faculty member in the Department of [Computer Science and Engineering](https://cse.iitpkd.ac.in/) at [IIT Palakkad](https://iitpkd.ac.in/). I completed my MTech+PhD dual degree in Communication Engineering from the Department of [Electrical Engineering](https://www.ee.iitb.ac.in/web) of [Indian Institute of Technology Bombay](https://www.iitb.ac.in/) in 2023. I was advised by [Prof. Parag Chaudhuri](https://www.cse.iitb.ac.in/~paragc/) and [Prof. Subhasis Chaudhuri](https://www.ee.iitb.ac.in/~sc/). Earlier, I completed my B.E. in [Electronics & Telecommunication Engineering](http://www.jaduniv.edu.in/view_department.php?deptid=84) from [Jadavpur University](http://www.jaduniv.edu.in/), Kolkata in 2015. My research interests include computer graphics, physics-based simulation, geometry processing, neural rendering, haptics, and XR.

name: Dr. B Sridharan
biosketch:
**Expertise**

**Dr. B. Sridharan** is working in computational and experimental hydraulics with a focus on free surface flow modeling. His research covers urban flooding, dam breaks, levee breaches, storm surges, and coastal and estuarine dynamics. He developed the Integrated River and Ocean Modeling System (IROMS), which is capable of simulating fluvial, pluvial, and coastal floods. His IROMS-Fluvial-Pluvial model is ten times more efficient than existing models, enabling real-time flood forecasting for Chennai. His IROMS-surge model accurately simulates coastal flooding along the Indian coast. Dr. Sridharan has also contributed to the hydrological and hydraulic assessments at Chennai Airport, identifying the causes of the 2015 floods and recommending improvements to the stormwater drainage system and compound walls. In Kerala's Kuttanad region, his involvement in hydraulic studies identified optimal sites for flood regulators and proposed new channels to divert water through the Thottappally spillway. Dr. B. Sridharan specializes in stormwater modeling and drainage network performance analysis in Chennai. As a hydraulic model review expert for the city's real-time flood forecasting system, he provides critical insights. He remains active in teaching, research, and consultancy, with publications in top journals.

**Experience**

Assistant Professor, Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Palakkad (19 Aug 2024 to Present)

name: Dr. C. V. Veena Venudharan
biosketch: Before joining IIT Palakkad as a faculty, Dr. Veena Venudharan worked as Research Manager in the Transportation Infra Business of Larsen and Toubro Construction. Dr. Veena was involved in the development of a Research Center which focused on Pavement Materials. Other responsibilities included technical audits of projects, practice of new technologies and troubleshooting at the site. Prior to that, she served as Project Associate at IIT Madras and IIT Kharagpur. She holds a Ph.D. in the area of Pavement Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur. Her Master is in Transportation Engineering, also from IIT Kharagpur. She holds a Bachelor degree in civil engineering from the University of Calicut. Her research interests include transportation pavement materials, design, maintenance, and rehabilitation; forensic evaluation; life cycle assessment; and sustainable infrastructure. She serves as a reviewer in many international journals and is a member of various professional bodies.

name: Dr. Chaitanya Paramatmuni
biosketch:
Dr. Chaitanya Paramatmuni, a mechanical engineer trained in mechanics of materials for manufacturing digital twins, joined Department of Mechanical Engineering in December 2024. Before joining the institute he was a postdoctoral research associate within the SAFER program at The University of Sheffield, United Kingdom. He pursued doctoral studies on micromechanics of defect nucleation and growth in magnesium alloys at Imperial College London, UK (2020) and M.S. (by research) at IIT Madras, India (2017).

He is an active professional member of the Institute of Materials, Minerals & Mining (IoM3) and is recognized as an Associate Fellow of Higher Education Academy (AFHEA) for his teaching skills that comply with UK professional framework standards. He enjoys trekking, food and music.
additional-information:

Opportunities

Description

Please do not hesitate to get in touch if you are interested to understand the Mechanical Behaviour of materials during/after metal forming and plastic deformation using Computations (Finite Element and Fast Fourier transforms based methods) and Experiments.

1. Interns: The institute offers Summer Internship program for 3rd and 4th year B-Tech students - details in https://sun.iitpkd.ac.in/

2. MTech/MS: Please check the HTTA/HTRA option

3. PhD: In addition to HTRA, the PMRF is a great opportunity - https://iitpkd.ac.in/notifications/prime-ministers-research-fellowships-pmrf

4. Postdocs: Check out the Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship - https://iitpkd.ac.in/research-programs-0

name: Dr. Chakradhar Dupadu
biosketch: Dr. Chakradhar Dupadu is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology, Palakkad. His research interests are sustainable machining, non-traditional machining, and artificial intelligence in manufacturing. He teaches Automation in Manufacturing for UG students; Digital Manufacturing and Soft Computing courses for PG students. Before joining IIT Palakkad, he worked as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Karnataka, Surathkal for four and half years.  He has also worked as a senior technology executive at Crompton Greaves Global R&D Centre Mumbai for a period of two years.

biosketch: I hold a B.Tech. degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur and a *Diploma de Estudios Avanzados (DEA; Diploma of Advanced Studies)* from Universitat Rovira i Virgili, Spain. I earned my Ph.D. from Pennsylvania State University under the supervision of Dr. Herschel A. Elliott. Following my doctoral work, I was a postdoctoral researcher in Dr. Stephen P. Long’s laboratory at the University of Illinois Urbana–Champaign (UIUC) and at UNICAMP, Brazil. Before joining IIT Palakkad as a faculty member, I also served as a Research Scientist at UIUC.

name: Dr. G Shrikanth Reddy
biosketch:
Dr. G. Shrikanth Reddy did his Ph.D. in the area of RF and Microwave component (passive) design in the year 2015 from Dept. of  Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bombay, India. He was also a Raman-Charpak fellow at Electronics and Electromagnetism Laboratory (L2E) UPMC Sorbonne Université where he worked under supervision of Prof. Julien toward identification of propagative modes within human tissue. In early 2016 Dr. Shrikanth joined State University of New York (Oswego) campus as a Post Doctoral Research Scientist, where he worked towards development of Vehicular antennas for covert applications. From  30th Nov 2016 to 3rd Sep. 2025 he was associated with Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Mandi as a faculty in School of Computing and Electrical Engineering. At IIT Mandi Dr. Shrikanth worked in diverse research areas such as: use of Frequency selective surface (FSS) for a) EM wave absorbers, b) RF energy harvester etc, design of electrically small antennas for wireless devices, development of EM sensors for medical applications etc. He also led interdisciplinary projects at IIT Mandi such as: Development of Artificial skin for robotic applications, and development of EM sensor for identification of a) different stages of Alzheimer's, b) Cancer growth etc., In academics he was associated with both UG and PG courses like EM theory and techniques, Antennas and wave propagation and Passive RF component design. He also held multiple administrative positions at IIT Mandi which includes Chair-Student Advisory Panel-Gymkhana Affairs (Asso. Dean Students),  Chief warden (Asso. Dean-Hostel Affairs), and Chaired multiple departmental committees. During his tenure at IIT Mandi he supervised and graduated 2 PhD, 2 MS and 8 M.tech students. 

Since 4th Sep. 2025 Dr. Shrikanth joined Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Palakkad as Associate Professor in the Dept. of Electrical Engineering. His current research interest includes design and analysis of RF and Microwave components such as Frequency selective surfaces (FSS), electrically small antennas, THz based meta-surface & their applications and MIMO configurations. Dr. Shrikanth is a Senior member of IEEE and also serves as an affiliated member in TC MTT-1 Field Theory and Computational EM Committee.

name: Dr. G Sujatha
biosketch:
Dr. G. Sujatha received her MA, MPhil, and Ph.D. in English from the University of Madras. Before joining the institute she worked as a lecturer in English in the Sree Sankaracharya University of Sanskrit for two years. She is specialized in gender, cultural and post-colonial studies.

She writes in English and Tamil and is involved in translation from English to Tamil and vice-versa.

name: Dr. Gopikrishnan Chirappurathu Remesan
biosketch:
### Education

* I completed my integrated Bachelor of Science-Master of Science (BS-MS dual degree) at the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Thiruvananthapuram (2010-2015). My major was Mathematics, with physics as my minor. 📚
* During this time, I also had the privilege of sharing knowledge as a guest lecturer at Bishop Chulaparambil Memorial (BCM) College for Women in Kottayam. Teaching and inspiring young minds—what a rewarding experience! 🌱
* My intellectual pursuits reached their zenith when I embarked on a captivating PhD journey. In 2016, I joined the IITB-Monash Research Academy, and after years of dedication and exploration, I defended my dissertation on **18 June 2021**. 🎓

### Postdoctoral Adventures

* The postdoctoral phase beckoned, and I eagerly answered the call. From July to December 2021, I donned the hat of an institute postdoctoral research fellow at the illustrious Department of Mathematics, IIT Bombay. The thrill of discovery and collaboration fueled my days! 🔬
* But wait, there’s more! In the picturesque city of Milan, I continued my academic escapades. Under the guidance of Prof. Andreas Veeser, I delved into fascinating research from **January 2022 to February 2023**. The cobbled streets of Milan echoed with mathematical musings! 🌆

### Present and Beyond

* Fast forward to the present—I now proudly serve as an Assistant Professor (Grade I) at the vibrant IIT Palakkad. Since **1 March 2023**, I’ve been nurturing young minds, igniting curiosity, and fostering a love for mathematics. 🌈

Remember, every equation you solve, every theorem you prove, and every student you inspire adds a splash of colour to the canvas of academia. Keep shining, keep exploring, and keep spreading the joy of mathematics! 🌟📊✨

name: Prof. Jagadeesh Bayry
biosketch:
Prof. Jagadeesh Bayry is a graduate of Veterinary Medicine from Bengaluru (1996) with a specialization of Virology and Immunology from Indian Veterinary Research Institute (1999). He received PhD in Immunology from Sorbonne University, Paris in 2003. Following post-doctoral research at University of Oxford, he joined Institut National de la Santé et de la Recherche Médicale (INSERM, The National Institute of Health and Medical Research), Paris, France in 2006 as a tenured scientist. In 2018, he became Director of Research at INSERM. In 2021, he joined IIT Palakkad as a Professor of Biological Sciences and Engineering. Currently he is a Head of the Department of Biological Sciences & Engineering. He has served as Dean (Student Affairs) at IIT Palakkad from January 2022 to March 2024.

His research is aimed at fundamental Immunology, autoimmunity, inflammation, immunotherapy and host-pathogen interaction. Prof. Bayry has obtained 38 National, European, International, Industry and Research Foundation grants. His h-index is 77 and has authored more than 320 articles in diverse international journals including Nature, New England Journal of Medicine, Science Immunology, Lancet Respiratory Medicine, Blood, Journal of Allergy & Clinical Immunology, Nature Communications, PNAS, Nature Reviews, Journal of Infectious Diseases, mBio, Trends Journals, and many others with >20,000 citations (**THE LINK TO FULL LIST OF PUBLICATIONS**: <https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/myncbi/jagadeesh.bayry.1/bibliography/public/>). He has many patents, has delivered more than 100 talks in prestigious institutions and conferences, and has guided more than 43 doctoral, post-doctoral and master fellows.

Prof. Bayry served as a member of a commission 'LS6-Immunity, Infection and Immunotherapy' at the European Research Council (ERC) for the consolidated ERC grants (2016 to 2022) and for the qualitative evaluation of completed ERC-funded projects (2022 and 2023). He has also served as an Advisory committee member for the Dutch Research Council.Prof. Bayry has uninterruptedly featured in the list of the **Top 2% Scientists** of the world (both in career-long data and single recent year data) including the latest one (Version 8, 19th September 2025, [https://elsevier.digitalcommonsdata.com/datasets/btchxktzyw/8](https://elsevier.digitalcommonsdata.com/datasets/btchxktzyw/6)) released by John P.A. Ioannidis of Stanford University in association with Elsevier. He has been elected as a Fellow of Academy of Translational Medicine Professionals, Austria (**FAcadTM, 2016**), Fellow of Indian Association of Veterinary Microbiologists, Immunologists and Specialists in Infectious Diseases (**FIAVMI, 2024**), Fellow of Indian Society of Veterinary Immunology and Biotechnology (**FISVIB, 2024**), Fellow of Academy for Environment and Life Sciences (**FAELS, 2025**), Fellow of Society for Applied Biotechnology (**FSAB, 2025**), Fellow of Indian Academy of Biomedical Sciences (**FIABS, 2025**), Fellow of Indian College of Allergy, Asthma and Applied Immunology (**FICAAI, 2025**), Fellow of Biotech Research Society of India (**FBRS, 2025**), Fellow of Indian Virological Society (**FIVS, 2025**), Fellow of the Royal Society of Biology, London, UK (**FRSB, 2025**) and Fellow of National Academy of Biological Sciences (**FNABS, 2026**). Prof. Bayry is serving/ has served as an Editor, Associate Editor or Editorial Board member of various journals like Frontiers in Immunology, Cellular & Molecular Immunology, Immunobiology, Discovery Medicine, Scientific Reports, Immunotherapy, Antibodies, PLoS ONE, Frontiers in Molecular Medicine, Virusdisease, and has edited four books from Springer Publisher.

Title

WORK EXPERIENCE

Description

**2021-**: Professor at Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, Palakkad, India

**2018-**: Director of Research (Equivalent of Professor) at Institut National de la Santé et de la Recherche Médicale (INSERM), Paris, France

**2010**-**2018**:  Chargé de Recherche-Classe Normale/CR1 (Equivalent of Associate Professor) at INSERM, Paris, France

**2006-2010**:  Chargé de Recherche-CR2 (Equivalent of Assistant Professor) at INSERM, Paris, France                       

**2004-2006**:  Postdoctoral Scientist, The Edward Jenner Institute for Vaccine Research, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK

**2003-2004**:  Engineer-Research, INSERM, Paris, France

**1999-2000**:  Research Assistant, Poultry Diagnostic and Research Center, Venkateshwara Hatcheries Pvt Ltd, Pune, India

Title

EDUCATION

Description

* **2010**:               Habilitation à Diriger des Recherches (**HDR**, Habilitation to Direct Research), Université de Paris (Paris Descartes University), Paris, France
* **2003**:               Doctorate in Immunology (Physiology and Physiopathology) (**PhD**), Sorbonne Université (Pierre and Marie Curie University), Paris, France
* **1999**:               Master of Veterinary Science (**MVSc**) in Virology and Immunology. Indian Veterinary Research Institute, Izatnagar/Bengaluru, India
* **1996**:               Bachelor of Veterinary Science (**BVSc**), Veterinary College - University of Agricultural Sciences, Bengaluru, India

Title

Description

**2022 & 2023:**Member of a commission at **European Research Council (ERC): Qualitative evaluation of completed ERC-funded projects 2009-2013**

**2021:**Advisory committee member for the **Dutch Research Council**

**2015:**Scientific Council Member: **INNO INDIGO** partnership Programme on “Diagnostic  and interventions in chronic non-communicable diseases”: Innvovation-driven          initiative for the development and integration of Indian and European research (India and Belgium, Estonia, France, Germany, Norway, Portugal)

name: Dr. Jaikrishnan Janardhanan
biosketch: I completed my B.Sc (Hons.) degree in Mathematics from Sathya Sai Institute of Higher Learning in 2007. I then joined the Integrated Ph.D. program at the Indian Institute of Science Bangalore. I worked under the supervision of Prof. Gautam Bharali and obtained my Ph.D in 2014. My thesis entitled "On the Structure of Proper Holomorphic Mappings" was awarded the Sir Martin Forster medal for the best thesis in Mathematical Sciences for the year 2013-14 by the Indian Institute of Science in July 2015. I was awarded the INSPIRE Facultyship in January 2015. I joined the Indian Institute of Technology Madras as an INSPIRE Hosted Faculty in April 2015. I spent the next four years at IIT Madras teaching a variety of courses. During this period, I was also an instructor for several workshops intended for both students as well as college teachers. I enjoy teaching and I hope to make useful contributions to the Math Circles planned by the Department of Mathematics.

name: Dr. Koninika Pal
department: Data Science, Computer Science And Engineering
biosketch: I am an assistant professor in the Department of Data Science and Computer Science & Engineering at IIT Palakkad. Before joining here, I was a postdoctoral fellow in [Prof. Gerhard Weikum](https://people.mpi-inf.mpg.de/~weikum/)'s group at Max Planck Institute for Informatics, Saarbruecken Germany. I received my Ph.D. degree from Technical University Kaiserslautern under the supervision of [Prof. Sebastian Michel](https://dbis.informatik.uni-kl.de/index.php/en/people/michel) in 2018.

name: Dr. Krishna Sesha Giri
biosketch:
My major interests are in teaching and research activities centred broadly in Fluid-thermal area. I offer UG and PG courses to students across various semesters. name: Dr. M. Ashok Kumar
biosketch: Dr Ashok Kumar received his bachelor's and master's degrees in Mathematics from the Manonmaniam Sundaranar University, Tirunelveli, in 1999 and 2001 respectively. He passed the CSIR-UGC (NET) for *lectureship* in Mathematical Sciences in 2001 and started lecturing Mathematics in various educational institutions until December 2007. During this period he developed an interest in Applied Mathematics. He obtained the CSIR-UGC (NET) for *JRF* in December 2007 and joined for a Ph.D programme in the department of Electrical Communication Engineering of the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, under the supervision of Prof. Rajesh Sundaresan. He completed his Ph.D in October 2014. Subsequently, he was a Visiting Scientist at the Stat-Math Unit of the Indian Statistical Institute, Bangalore from December 2014 to May 2015 and a post-doctoral fellow at the department of Electrical Engineering of the Technion-Israel Institute of Technology from May 2015 to December 2015 under the mentorship of Prof. Igal Sason. He was with the discipline of Mathematics of IIT Indore from January 2016 to May 2018. He has been with the department of Mathematics, IIT Palakkad since June 2018.

name: Dr. M. Sabarimalai Manikandan
biosketch:
**Educational Qualification and Experiences**: M. Sabarimalai Manikandan received the B.E. degree in ECE from Bharathiar University, India, the M.E. degree in Microwave and Optical Engineering from Madurai Kamaraj University, India, and the Ph.D. degree in Biosignal Processing from IIT Guwahati, India. He was an Assistant Professor (Jul 2013 - Dec 2021) at School of Electrical Sciences, IIT Bhubaneswar. He was a Chief Engineer (Dec 2011 - July 2013) with the Advanced Technology Group, Samsung India Electronic Pvt. Ltd., Noida, India. He was an Assistant Professor (Jan 2010 - Dec 2011) at Center for Computational Engineering and Networking (CEN), Amrita University, India and a Lead Engineer (Jan 2009 - Jan 2010) with the Audio Group, Samsung India Electronic Pvt. Ltd., Noida, India.

**Main Research Focus**: Design and development of Energy-Efficient AI-powered Health Sensing and Monitoring Systems, Drone Detection and See-Through Wall Human Sensing, Human-Machine Interactions and Voice-Enabled Technologies, and Sound Based Surveillance Systems.

**Professional Activities and Recognition Awards:** Dr. M. S. Manikandan was a **recipient of the 2012 Outstanding Performance Award** and also **Employee of the Month** during his tenure at **Samsung India Electronic Pvt., Ltd.** and received **2019 CVET Most Cited Article Award** at the Biomedical Engineering Society (BME) conference, Philadelphia, USA and 2019 **IET Healthcare Technology Letters Premium Award**. He is currently the **Chair of Cardio–Respiratory (CR) Subgroup of IEEE Standards P1752.2** and **Vice Chair of Working Group of IEEE Standards P2520.3.1 (Standard for Machine Olfaction Devices and Systems)** and **Member of Working Group of IEEE P1924.1** (Energy Efficient Communication Hardware (COM/GreenICT-SC/EECH)) with main contribution of Recommendation of Sensor and Data Acquisition Techniques. He serves as Associate Editor of IEEE Access, Healthcare Technology Letters, IET Generation, Transmission & Distribution, PLOS Digital Health and Frontiers in Signal Processing (Biomedical Signal Processing) and Academic Editor and Guest Editor of Journal of Healthcare Engineering. He serves as the reviewer for reputed journals including the IEEE, ACM, IET, Springer, Elsevier, PLOS ONE, and Taylor and Francis.

**Technical Expert Committe Member and Project Proposal Reviewer**: SERB Proposal Reviewer: Odisha Startup Application Evaluation and Selection and Fund Approval; Startup Policy Development;  Technology Development Board (TDB), DST; Technical Member, Procurement, Department of Women & Child Development and Mission Shakti; Testing of GPS PDAs, Forest Hqrs. Odisha; Technical Equipment Procurement, Odisha Police-Special Operation Group; Technical Evaluation /field trial of UAV, SOG Hdqrs, Odisha; Translation of Mandarin to English and vice versa, Technical Consultative Workshop under Technology Development Fund (TDF) Scheme; Mobility Plan Co-ordination Committee (MPCC) for Low Carbon Mobility Plan (LCMP), Bhubaneswar Development Authority;  and Technical Evaluation, Mass Communication & Early Warning Dissemination System, Odisha Space Applications Centre (ORSAC).

**Institute Administrative Responsibilities (Past at IIT Bhubaneswar):** 

**Director**, IITBBS Research and Entrepreneur Park -One of the Directors, **Professor In Charge (PIC) of Startup Centre**, **Joint PIC of Permanent Campus**, **PIC of Horticulture**; **In-charge of** **Security Services**;  **Coordinator**: Odisha Startup Nodal Officer, National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) Coordinator,  Institute Ethical Committee (IEC)-Health; **Member of**: House Allotment Committee, Women Welfare Committee (WWC), Unnat Bharat Abhiyan, Technology Market Committee (TMC), Procurement Committee, Auction/Tender Committee, Convocation and Institute Foundation Day; **Selection Committee Member of**: Horticulturist, Junior Technical Superintendent & Junior Technician, Security Agency and **Assessment Committee Member:** Review the Performance of Non-Teaching Staff, and Satff Confirmation Committee Member.

name: Dr. Manas Kumar Jena
biosketch:
Dr. Manas Kumar Jena received his Ph.D. in Power System Engineering from the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, India.  Currently, he holds the position of Associate Professor at the department of electrical engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, India. Before joining IIT Palakkad, he served as a Subject Matter Expert (SME), at General Electric (GE-Power). He is a Senior Member of IEEE and holds the position of IEEE PES SB Advisor at IIT Palakkad. He is the recipient of the Indian National Academy of Engineers (INAE) Innovative Student Projects Award 2018- Doctoral Level, POSOCO Power System Award (PPSA)-2019, GE-Impact Award-2019 for the development and delivery of PhasorAnalytics Software Solution to ONS-Brazil, the Indian National Academy of Engineers (INAE) Young Engineer Award 2021, and IIT Palakkad Best Teacher Award 2025. He is an associate editor of the IET Generation, Transmission, and Distribution Journal. 

His major research interests include the application of Synchrophasor Technology to enhance Power Grid Monitoring, Control, and Protection, Development of WAMS Grid Stability Solutions, Adaptive protection of transmission system, etc.

name: Dr. Manav Khaire
biosketch:
I am a public policy researcher interested in analyzing social policy design and outcomes in India. I have an M.Phil. (Planning and Development) and Ph.D. (Public Policy) from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IITB). With a degree in Electrical Engineering, I have worked in the industry for seven years across multinational companies like Philips Analytical and the Electronics Corporation of India Limited (ECIL) before pursuing a master's degree from IITB.

My research area primarily engages with the political economy of housing, examining the state-market-housing interrelations. In my research, I use an interdisciplinary approach along with policy analysis research methods to engage with critical analysis of social policies. Going forward, I hope to collaborate with researchers across disciplines regarding the matters of policy analysis and policy design in respective sectors of interest.
teaching: For undergraduates, I offer a course on Public Policy for Engineers, which informs students about the basics of public policy and various tools of policy evaluation. For master's students, I offer courses on core courses related to Urban Policy, Technology and Policy, Public Policy in India, Gender and Policy, Policy Analysis, and Development Planning in India, among others. For PhD students, I  offer specialized and advanced courses on research methodology focusing on quantitative, qualitative, and mixed methods research design.

name: Prof. Mayank Tiwari
biosketch:
**Professor IIT Palakkad 18th June 2025 todate**

**Visiting Professor – IIT Palakkad  15th July 2024 to 17th June 2025**

**Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Patna, February 2022 to 17th June 2025**

Head Department of Mechanical Engineering IIT Patna  from  March 2023 to March 2024

Chair Professor Bureau of Indian Standards 26th May 2023 to May 2024

 Member of  BIS ETD 42 committee for Wind Turbines

**Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Patna, September 2013 to February 2022**

1.    Associate Dean Faculty Affairs   July 2021 to date  
2.    Member BoG IIT Patna Senate Nominee from 2018 till 2020

•    **Postdoctoral Visiting Research Scholar, Acoustics and Dynamic Laboratory The Ohio State University USA  July 1999 to 2001  
•    Project Associate, 1999, Industrial Tribology Machine Dynamics & Maintenance Engineering Centre, IIT Delhi.** Analysis of a Turbopump rotor theoretical analysis (FEM) of a high speed Turbopump rotor- steady state and transient unbalance response   
**•    Senior Research Assistant, 1991-1995, Vibrations Research Laboratory, Department of  Mechanical Engineering, IIT Delhi Aeroengine rotor dynamics-** Experimental validation of transfer Matrix based rotor dynamics code for 2 spool gas turbine rotor, development of a squeeze film damper  
**•    Graduate Engineer Trainee 1987-89, Tata Engineering Locomotive Company, Jamshedpur, INDIA: Quality Assurance Division of the Axle machining plant.**

**Work Experience**

In Industry (General Electric) worked on development of dynamic systems such as X-Ray tube rotor bearing, motors and drive trains of Wind turbines. The development of X Ray tube rotors analysed novel bearing systems for the motor and anode. Liquid metal Lubricant was tested out as lubricant for the high vacuum and temperature conditions. This has resulted in patents and publications in USA. Currently working on enhancing performance of systems such as Wind Turbine and EV drive train by reducing bearing friction. Also working on systems for Aircraft Engines for introducing high material damping and analyzing nonlinear response of such systems

BIS Chair Professor in IIT Patna and member BIS committee on Wind Turbines

Working on societal relevance projects under Swacchta Action Plan for which Designs and Patents have been granted in  
India

Administrative positions including Senate nominee to BoG of IIT Patna and led Department of  
Mechanical Engineering, Research & Development unit and Faculty affairs IIT Patna
teaching:
**Academic Institute teaching experience of 12years:** Engineering Design, Machine Design, Dynamics, Vehicle Dynamics, Rotordynamics, Mechatronics, and Tribology

**Industry and Postdoctoral teaching experience of  11 years**: Vibrations, X Ray Tube Dynamics, Controls

name: Dr. Mintu Porel
biosketch:
### Experiences

* Associate Professor (2023-Present): [Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad](http://turing.iitpkd.ac.in/web/), Kerala, India
* Assistant Professor (2017-2023): [Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad](http://turing.iitpkd.ac.in/web/), Kerala, India
* Postdoctoral research (2014-2017): [Cornell University](https://www.cornell.edu/), New York, USA
* Postdoctoral research (2013-2014): [Columbia University](http://www.columbia.edu/), New York, USA

### Educations

* Ph.D. in Chemistry (2007-2012): [University of Miami](http://www.as.miami.edu/chemistry/), Florida, USA
* M.Sc. in Chemistry (2005-2007): [Indian Institute of Technology Delhi](http://chemistry.iitd.ac.in/), New Delhi, India
* B.Sc. in Chemistry (2002-2005): [University of Calcutta](http://www.caluniv.ac.in/), West Bengal, India

### Visiting Research Experiences

* [Columbia University](http://www.columbia.edu/), USA (May 2010 and August 2011)
* [Rutgers University](https://www.rutgers.edu/), USA (Jan 2011)
* [Case Western Reserve University](https://case.edu/), USA (April 2012-September 2012)

Description

### Research Project

1. Title: Design and Synthesis of a Novel Class of Sequence-defined Macrocycles with Modular Post-synthetic Modification for Material and Biomedical Applications

Funding source: Core Research Grant (CRG), DST, India

Amount: 53.65 lakhs  
Role: PI  
Status: 2020-2023

2. Title: Study of the interaction between synthetic sequence-defined macromolecules and lipid-membrane towards developing antibacterial and anticancer drug

Funding source: MHRD-STARS, India  
Amount: 99.7 lakhs  
Role: PI  
Status: 2020-2023, ongoing

3. Title: A Novel Class of Functionally Controlled Macromolecules with Tunable Properties for Material and Biomedical Applications

Funding source: Ramanujan Fellowship, DST-SERB, India  
Amount: 38 lakhs  
Role: PI  
Status: 2018-2023, ongoing

name: Dr. Nelson Muthu
biosketch: *Dr. Nelson Muthu is a product of three well-known universities: IIT Guwahati where he did his B.Tech in 2008, IIT Bombay and Monash University (IITB-Monash Research Academy) where he completed his PhD, in 2015, on studies on element-free Galerkin method for linear elastic fracture mechanics problems. Prior to PhD, he had corporate experience as an analyst in Global Analytics, Chennai from July'08-July'10, where he learnt predictive analytics. After his PhD, he worked in Biomedical Engineering and Technology incubation Centre (BETiC), IIT Bombay on medical device innovation from Dec'15 to May'17. Afterward, he worked in IIT Guwahati as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering from May'17 to Dec'23. His current research areas include fracture and damage mechanics, mechanics of composite materials, meshfree methods, elasticity, and bio-medical device innovation.*

Research associate with Professor Dr. Dominik Marx. Work was mainly focused on calculating and simulating the mechanochemistry of ring opening reactions and mechanochemical bond breaking mechanisms of protein disulfides. Expertize in QM/MM, Metadynamics and Advanced sampling techniques.

teaching:
Joined IIT Palakkad from July 2016 onwards as Assistant Professor and since then, I have been offering Chemistry Theory Courses and  Chemistry Lab Courses for both UG and PG students.

• CY1011 (CY1010 previously): This course mainly deals with Thermodynamics, Kinetics and Organic Chemistry and this is UG level course for the B. Tech students.

• CY1021 (CY1020 previously): This is a UG level core course for the B. Techs which mainly focuses on Theoretical and Inorganic Chemistry.

• CY1140 (CY1030 previously): This is an undergraduate lab course for the B. Tech Students.

• CY5005: This is a PG level core course and this course mainly incorporates Symmetry, Group Theory and Spectroscopy.

• CY5103: This is an advanced level lab course for the PG students and in here, we have introduced introduction and hands on problems in Computational Chemistry for the UG students.

• CY3601: This elective course mainly associates with Electrochemistry and Corrosion and is offered for the 5th and 7th semester B. Tech students.

• CY5609: This is an advanced level Quantum Chemistry course which incorporates both the theory as well as the lab and is offered mainly oriented towards the PG community.

Apart from these, I have been actively offering the internships and project (internal/external) for the UG and PG students.

name: Dr. Rahul Choragudi
biosketch: Dr. Rahul is a trained sociologist and an ethnographer. He finished his doctoral degree from the Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai in 2016. His thesis explored the dialectics of dual hierarchy and its metamorphosis since independence in rural Andhra Pradesh. Prior to joining IIT Palakkad, he was an Assistant Professor at the School of Development, Azim Premji University from 2021 and an Assistant Professor at the Institute of Public Policy, National Law School of India University, Bengaluru from 2016 to 2021.

name: Dr. Ram V G
biosketch:
Dr. Ram V.G. holds a B.E. in Civil Engineering from the College of Engineering Guindy, Anna University, and an M.S. and Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Construction Management) from IIT Madras. His doctoral research focused on identifying and mitigating material management inefficiencies in the Indian construction sector.

Prior to joining IIT Palakkad, he worked as a Research Associate at the University of Sheffield (UK), where he was involved in multiple EU Horizon Europe–funded projects. His postdoctoral research examined the socio-economic impacts of circular economy (CE) practices in the construction sector, including rebound effects, as well as their contribution to climate change mitigation, particularly in carbon-intensive supply chains such as steel, glass, and cement.

His research at IIT Palakkad focuses on socio-technical construction systems and productivity, with particular emphasis on human–technology interaction, including exoskeletons and other assistive technologies. His work examines how technological, organisational, and systemic interventions influence project performance, worker well-being, and the long-term sustainability of construction systems.
teaching: CE 3014 Construction Scheduling and Control (Jan-May 2026) (Core - BTech)

name: Dr. Reenu Punnoose
biosketch: Reenu received her B.A. degree in English from Stella Maris College (affiliated to the University of Madras) in 2005, her M.A. in Applied Linguistics from the University of York (U.K) in 2007 and her PhD in Linguistics from Newcastle University (U.K) in 2011. She taught briefly in the Linguistics department at the Central University of Kerala in the 2013-2014 academic year. She was awarded a three year UGC Post-Doctoral Fellowship in 2015, the Dr. S. Radhakrishnan Post-Doctoral Fellowship in Humanities and Social Sciences (including languages), which she took up at the Centre for Linguistics at the Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi.

Workshop Coordinated and Organized

Description

* “**Essential Techniques for Research Problems in Materials and Manufacturing (ETRPMM 2014)**” during 14th -18th April 2014 at Mechanical Engineering Department in NIT Warangal.

Kanmani Subbu** in Department of Mechanical Engineering, NIT Warangal during 17th Sep. 2018 to 22nd Sep., 2018.  (GIAN Expert: Dr Sri Bandyopadhaya, School of Materials Science & Engineering, UNSW Australia, Kensington Campus)
* “**Hybrid Additive Manufacturing Viewed from Materials Science: Fundamentals and State of the Art**” M J Davidson, and **S. Kanmani Subbu** in Department of Mechanical Engineering, NIT Warangal during 3rd April 2019 to 7th April  2019 (GIAN Expert: Surendar K. Marya, Ecole Centrale Nantes, France)

Title

Academic Responsibilities

Description

* II/IV B.Tech. (Mechanical Engineering) Faculty coordinator for two years (2014-2106).
* Active member in NBA accreditation team– B.Tech. & M.Tech. (Mfg. Engg, and CIM), Mech. Engg. .
* Mentor for one EPICS project of 5 final year students from various departments.
* Faculty advisor for NITW Go Kart Team-2014.
* Mentor for 2 students of Mechanical Engineering from NIT Mizoram under Ishan Vikas Program
* Member of Industrial and Academic Liasons Committee

Title

Achievements and Laurels

Description

* Received Second prize, Poster presentation, ASME Gas Turbine, Bangalore, India Dec. 7-8, 2017
* Top 25 Hottest Articles, Procedia Engineering, 2015
* M.E (Manufacturing Engineering) Gold Medalist 2004-2006, Anna University.
* DST and CSIR travel grant received for international conference presentations.
* Received cash award from IIT Kanpur for SCI indexed Journal publications.
* Participated in events like Mathematics quiz and Technical quiz.
* Participated and solved Rubik's cube in Techkriti’13 for Guinness world record in IIT Kanpur.
* Secured Second position in BE Mechanical Engineering (1999-2003).
* Delivered Expert Lectures in IIT Kanpur, VNIT Nagpur, S.V University Tirupati, A.C. Tech. Karaikudi, NIT Warangal, and VIT Vellore.
* Advisory Committee Member: International and National conferences.

name: Dr. Sandeep Chandran
biosketch: Sandeep Chandran is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Palakkad. He received his PhD from IIT Delhi where he received the IIT Delhi FITT award for Best Industry Relevant PhD. Prior to joining the Institute, he was a Senior Design Engineer at AMD where he was part of the performance modelling team that designed the Zen microarchitecture. He was also a Research Intern in the Silicon Bring-up Team at Freescale Semiconductors (now NXP semiconductors). His research interests are in areas of design and verification of high-performance systems.

name: Prof. Santhakumar Mohan
biosketch:
**Santhakumar Mohan** got his Ph.D. (Robotics and Control) from the Indian Institute of Technology Madras, Chennai (India) in 2010. From June 2010 to March 2011, he worked as an assistant professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC), Kerala (India). He then worked as a postdoctoral fellow at the Korean Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST), Daejeon (Republic of Korea). In 2012, he joined the faculty of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Indore. He holds visiting faculty positions at IISc Bangalore, India, BSTU, Belgorod, Russia, KAIST, Daejeon, Republic of Korea, RWTH Aachen, Germany, and ECN, France. His active research areas include Service and Field Robots (specifically, the design & motion control of underwater and wheeled mobile robots), Parallel Robotic Platforms, and Assistive and Rehabilitation Robots. Furthermore, he has received the outstanding young Scientist for the year 2014 from the Korea Robotics Society, European Master on Advanced Robotics Plus (EMARO+) fellowship (2018-2019), Alexander von Humboldt (AvH) Fellowship (2016–2017), Satellite across virtual institutes (SAVI) fellowship (2013-2014), World-class university fellowship (2011- 2012) and Brian Korean 21 (BK21) fellowship (2011). He has published more than 150 articles in various journals and conference proceedings. He has authored one book, edited four books, completed fifteen externally funded projects, and is currently pursuing five sponsored projects.

He is associated with various journals as Technical Editor of *IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics* (2020–2025); as Associate Editor of *IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters* (2020–2024), *Advances in Mechanical Engineering* (2023–present), *ASME Letters in Dynamic Systems and Control* (2020–present), *Discover Robotics* (2025-present), *Intelligent Service Robotics* (2024–present), and *Robotica* (2024–present); and as Guest Editor of the *ASME Journal of Computing and Information Science in Engineering* (2024) and *Frontiers in Plant Science* (2025).

He is also an adjunct professor at the Department of Engineering Design at IIT Madras and a Faculty Fellow at the IIT Palakkad Technology Ihub Foundation (IPTIF).

He is the founding director of the IIT Palakkad Technology I-Hub Foundation ([IPTIF](https://iptif.tech/)) and the Technology Innovation Foundation of IIT Palakkad ([TECHIN](https://techin-iitpkd.org/)).

name: Dr. Senthilkumar. V
biosketch: Before joining IIT Palakkad as a faculty, Dr. Senthilkumar worked as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at the University of Sharjah Since Feb 2014. Prior to that, he worked in Academia, Research, and Industry in various capacities; he served as a Senior Lecturer in the School of Construction Economics and Management, under the Faculty of Engineering & the Built Environment at the University of the Witwatersrand, South Africa; Senior lecturer at Caledonian College of Engineering, Muscat, Oman; Senior Project Officer at IIT Madras, also involved in Design Interface coordination at Delhi International Airport Limited project. He completed doctoral research in the area of Construction Engineering and Management at the Indian Institute of Technology (Madras), India. His master's degree is in Structural Engineering and his bachelor's degree is in Civil Engineering, both from Thiagarajar College of Engineering, Madurai. At present, he teaches and conducts research in the area of Construction Engineering and Management. His specific research focus includes Design Management in Infrastructure Projects, Automation in Construction, Lean Construction, and Policy Making in Infrastructure Developmental Projects. He has a number of International Journal and Conference publications in the area of Construction Engineering and Management. He is a member of various professional bodies around the world. He represented India as an Expert in the World Skills Competition for "Digital Construction" Skills at the WorldSkills2022SE competition held in France.

name: Dr. Shanmugaraju Sankarasekaran
biosketch:
* **06/2023─Present:** Associate Professor in Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, Kerala.
* **10/2018─06/2023:** Assistant Professor in Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, Kerala.
* **11/2015─09/2018:** Senior Postdoctoral Fellow, Trinity Biomedical Sciences Institute (TBSI), Trinity College Dublin, Ireland.

* **11/2013─11/2015:** Irish Research Council (IRC) Postdoctoral Fellow, Trinity Biomedical Sciences Institute (TBSI), Trinity College Dublin, Ireland.

* **03/2013─11/2013:** Research Associate, Department of Inorganic and Physical Chemistry, Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, India.

* **08/2007─03/2013:** Ph.D. in Inorganic Chemistry (***Awarded Prof. S. Soundararajan Gold Medal for the Best Ph.D. Thesis***), Department of Inorganic and Physical Chemistry, Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, India.

* **06/2005─04/2007:** M.Sc. in Chemistry with First Class, The American College, Madurai, India.
* **06/2002─04/2005:** B.Sc. in Chemistry (Spl.) with First Class, The American College, Madurai, India.

* August 2007 ─ August 2009, Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) from the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), Government of India.

name: Dr. Soumya Mukherjee
biosketch:
Non-linear Constitutive Modeling

v I develop robust constitutive models (relations between stress and strain) for various nonlinear soft materials, including rubber, foam, biological tissues, the brain, arteries, etc. Our modeling often involves advanced tensor analysis, sophisticated experiments, as well as machine learning.

Mechanics of Residually Stressed Materials

v Residual stress has created a new arena in continuum mechanics. Almost all existing frameworks of continuum mechanics rely on the assumption that the material remains stress-free, before any load is applied. However, residual stress is unavoidably present in almost all real-life mechanical and biological structures, affecting the constitutive relations, mechanical behavior, failure, instabilities, buckling, wrinkling, etc. We are investigating these influences of residual stress on engineering and biological systems, which starts with identifying the appropriate constitutive relations in presence of residual stress.

v Our approach to accurately and exactly determine the constitutive relation for residually stressed materials, is not straight-forward, or trivial. It involves inverse analysis of the existing continuum mechanics theories. We have developed the first constitutive relations for residually stressed materials, exhibiting

            (a) [non-linear viscoelasticity](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmps.2024.105544),

            (b) [anisotropic elasticity](https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/full/10.1098/rspa.2022.0255),

            (c) [implicit elasticity](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0020722524000636), and

            (d) [limited stretchability](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0093641322000131).

          (e) We also developed the [first failure model](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S009364132200057X) for residually stressed soft materials. A generalization of this failure model can be found [here](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mechmat.2026.105598).

         (f) [Here](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijnonlinmec.2025.105070) is a paper on new approaches of changing reference configuration-- to identify the stress-free material from the mechanical behavior of the residually stressed body.    

v We are planning to create YouTube videos to explain our residual stress theory to the general audiences. The present publications are accessible via [google scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=uiBBBncAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=sra)or [researchgate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Soumya-Mukherjee-19?ev=hdr_xprf). We will soon report finite element investigations of wrinkling, buckling, bulging etc., using new models of residual stress developed by our team.  We have also determined a von Kármán theory for residually stressed plates, and currently pursuing the finite element analysis.

Continuum Mechanics

v I am actively working on implicit elasticity[[link](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijengsci.2024.104079), [link](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijnonlinmec.2025.105070), [link](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijengsci.2026.104500)], non-linear viscoelasticity [[link](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmps.2024.105544), [link](https://doi.org/10.1177/10812865231189807)], and developing non-linear [[link](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijsolstr.2025.113253)], or weakly non-linear plate theories.

name: Dr. Subrahmanyam Mula
biosketch:
Subrahmanyam Mula received the B.E. degree in electronics and communication engineering from Andhra University, Visakhapatnam in 2001, and the M.Tech. degree in microelectronics and VLSI design from IIT Kharagpur in 2003, and Ph.D. degree from Department of Electronics and Electrical Communication Engineering, IIT Kharagpur in 2018. From 2003 to 2014, he was with Intel, Bengaluru where he was involved in front-end design verification of gigabit Ethernet switches, processors, chip-sets, and GPUs. He supported six generations of Intel GPUs from Eaglelake (Gen4) on 65nm to Skylake (Gen9) on 14nm at various levels such as architecture, RTL, Verification, GLS, STA, DFT and Post Si Debug. His current research interests include VLSI architectures for real time signal processing applications and adaptive learning systems.  
.

* Organised an online lecture by  [Prof Urec Mojca, Dean & Associate Professor, Faculty of Social Work, University of Ljubljana](https://www.fsd.uni-lj.si/en/faculty/faculty/teachers_and_staff/2008050811511149/#:~:text=Mojca%20Urek%2C%20PhD%2C%20Associate%20Professor,e-mail%3A%20mojca.urek%40fsd.uni-lj.si&text=contact%20hours%3A) on  *Inclusive social and health care for LGBTI+ older people– Slovenian and European Union (EU) research perspectives,* 24 February, 2026.
* Resource person for the topic  Marginalities and Vulnerabilities as part of the Diploma in Integrated Mental Health Care (DIMHC), implemented by The Banyan Academy of Leadership in Mental Health (BALM) in collaboration with the Kudumbashree Mission, Nehru Yuva Kendra Hall, District Collectorate, Malappuram District, November 28, 2025.

* Granted publishing partnership of  Human Library Denmark (2025)
* Co-oragniser, UNCHAiN – Youth Ideathon to Combat Drug Abuse, as part of the efforts to promote awareness and gather practical, youth-driven ideas to combat drug abuse by the [District Administration Palakkad](https://palakkad.nic.in/en/) in association with UBA RCI IITPKD, [Kerala Police](https://keralapolice.gov.in/), [Kerala State Excise Department](https://keralaexcise.gov.in/),[Department of General Education](https://education.kerala.gov.in/) and[Social Justice Department,](https://sjd.kerala.gov.in/index_eng.php) Government of Kerala.
* Screening of the documentary, *Wounds of Pyre: Inside the minds of cremation workers* through UBA RCI IITPKD -MHI Mumbai-PDPL at Palakkad District Public Library, Palakkad, 30 May 2025
* *Can You See Me?” Re-imagining college mental health*, Ashtamgam Ayurveda Chikitsalayam and Vidyapeedham (Ayurveda College), Koottanad, Palakkad,  April  2025.
* Field visit to PHC as part of the HS 5009 course, March 22, 2025.

* One-Day Workshop on  Substance*Abuse Among Youth* for teachers and students of higher educational institutions  on March 2 (Sunday) from 9 a.m - 4 p.m at the IMA Hall [(Google map link](https://www.google.com/maps/place/Indian+Medical+Association,+Sultanpet,+Palakkad,+Kerala+678001/@10.7659766,76.6585752,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x3ba86def2b422daf:0xef58bff163414ecd!8m2!3d10.7659766!4d76.6585752!16s%2Fg%2F12hk_9x47?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDEyNi4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D)), Near Fort Maidan, Palakkad by  Unnat Bharat Abhiyan (UBA) Regional Coordinating Institute (RCI),  IIT Palakkad in association with Indian Medical Association (IMA) Palakkad branch, March 2, 2025.
* Online lecture by Dr Nikita Hari, Head of Teaching and Design Support Group (TDSG), Oxford University on August 7, 2024 as part of UBA  RCI IIT Palakkad lecture series.
* Online lecture by Prof Dr Rajagopal, Padma Sri awardee, former Professor of Anesthesiology, Government Medical College, Kozhikode and pioneer of palliative care movement in India  on June 25, 2024 as part of UBA IIT Palakkad lecture series ( ([Youtube link](https://www.youtube.com/live/TtasqonQHLw?feature=shared))

name: Dr. Sushabhan Sadhukhan
biosketch:
**Positions:**

* **Assistant Professor**  
  Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, India  
  Duration: July 2019 - May 2023

* **Assistant Professor**  
  Discipline of Biosciences & Biomedical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Indore, India  
  Duration: May 2017–June 2019

* **Postdoctoral Associate**  
  Department of Chemistry and Chemical Biology, Cornell University, New York, USA  
  Duration: Sept 2012 - April 2017

**Education:**

name: Dr. Swaroop Sahoo
biosketch:
**PROFESSIONAL TIMELINES**

July 2025 - now:Associate Professor (regular), Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, India

June 2017 – July 2025: Assistant Professor (regular), Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, India

July 2016 - June 2017: Assistant Professor (contract), Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, India

Feb 2016 – July 2016: Assistant Professor, Amrita University

Jan 2015 - Jan 2016: Assistant Professor, KIIT University

Nov 2005 – July 2007: Systems Engineering, Tata Consultancy Services

­**EDUCATION**

* **December 2014** Doctor of Philosophy, Colorado State University, Fort Collins, CO, CGPA – 3.54/4
* **May 2011**Master of Science, Colorado State University, Fort Collins, CO, CGPA – 3.47/4
* **July 2005** Bachelor of Technology, College of Engineering and Technology, *Biju Patnaik University of Technology,* Bhubaneswar, Orissa, *CGPA – 7.8/10*
* MS and PhD student in the group of Prof. Steven C. Reising at Colorado State University, August 2007 - December 2014
* PhD Dissertation: Retrieval Techniques and Information Content Analysis to Improve Remote Sensing of Atmospheric Water Vapor, Liquid Water and Temperature from Ground-based Microwave Radiometer Measurements
* MS Thesis: Increasing Vertical Resolution of Three-Dimensional Atmospheric Water Vapor Retrievals using a Network of Scanning Compact Microwave Radiometers

**RESEARCH EXPERIENCE**

* Development and fabrication of an S-band phased array antenna with reduced mutual coupling between elements as well as testing. Mutual coupling between antenna elements and the impact on antenna performance. (Journal paper [5] and [6])
* Design of C-band phase switching and frequency reconfigurable phased array antenna. The antenna element for the phased array has been fabricated and tested. The fabrication and testing of phased array is in progress (Conference paper [4])
* Distributed beamforming with beamforming techniques in phased array radar to improve accuracy of weather radar variables. (Journal paper under review)
* Weather radar signal processing for determining types of particles in cloud i.e., graupel, aggregates, hail, dendrites, plates etc. (Journal paper [1])
* Theoretical analysis of properties of radar signals scattered from various types of cloud particles i.e., graupel, aggregates, hail, dendrites, plates. (Core grant and conference paper [9])
* Detection of severe storm using weather radar measurements and signal processing. (Conference paper [3])
* Novel RFID tag antenna design and fabrication. (Journal paper [4])
* Human heart rate detection using mmwave radar measurements and signal processing. (Consultancy project and conference paper [10])
* Detecting the dielectric constant of water using in situ antenna measurements. (Journal paper [8])
* Detection of fighter aircraft in the presence of chaff in aerial battlefield using radar measurements. (Conference paper [2])

Motivational Philosophy

Being a good engineer is not always about having the best technology knowledge, that part can be taught. What can't be taught is integrity, tremendous work ethic and grit; that will definitely make you a good engineer no matter where you start.

teaching:
• **CY3601: Electrochemistry and Corrosion -** This elective course is mainly associated with Electrochemistry and Corrosion and is offered for third and fourth-year B. Tech students.

• **CY1140: Engineering Chemistry Laboratory -** This is an undergraduate lab course for the B. Tech Students.

• **CY5011: Dynamics of Charge transport** - This is an advanced level course associated with Electrode interfaces, Surface Chemistry, and Catalysis and it is offered to the PG community.

• **CY5002: Chemistry of Materials** - This is an advanced level material science course that is offered to the PG community.

name: Dr. Yuvaraj K
biosketch:
**Professional Details**

**Assistant Professor** [February 2023-Present]: Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad, Kerala, India.

**Post-Doctoral Fellow** [January 2019-January 2023]: Advisor: Prof. Cameron Jones, Monash University, Australia.

**SERB- Overseas Post-Doctoral Fellowship (O-PDF)** [January 2018-December 2018]:Advisor: Prof. Cameron Jones, Monash University, Australia.

**SERB- National Post-Doctoral Fellowship (N-PDF)** [August 2017-December 2017]: Advisor: Dr. Sakya S Sen, CSIR - National Chemical Laboratory (NCL), Pune.

**Institute Pre-Doctoral Fellow** [November 2016-May 2017]:Supervisor: Prof. Sundargopal Ghosh, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, India.

**Education Details**

2012-2017:  Ph.D. in Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, India.

2008-2010:  MSc in Chemistry, Bharathidasan University, Tamil Nadu, India.

2005-2008:  BSc in Chemistry (Major), Bharathiyar University, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India.

**Doctoral Details**

*Dissertation*: **Chemistry of Diruthenium Analogues of Tetraborane(10) and Pentaborane(9)**

*Supervisor*: **Prof. Sundargopal Ghosh**,Indian Institute of Technology Madras, India.
